title: Schefflera allocotantha
taxonomy: Schefflera allocotantha is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. This family consists of about 1,300 species of plants commonly known as ginsengs, ivies, and tree ferns. Schefflera allocotantha is native to tropical regions and is often used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive foliage. The plant is a member of the genus Schefflera, which comprises approximately 600 species of flowering plants. Schefflera allocotantha is a popular choice for indoor and outdoor gardens, valued for its ability to purify the air and its low-maintenance requirements.
